content.problemStatement:
Sasha is a little girl who likes to view and compare photos. In this problem, a photo is a grid of pixels with hhh rows and www columns; each cell of this grid is either black or white. We will call the ordered pair of integers (h,w)(h, w)(h,w) the shape of a photo.

Let's define the similarity of two photos PPP and RRR with **the same** shape (h,w)(h, w)(h,w) as the number of pairs of indices i,ji, ji,j (1‚Č§i‚Č§h1 \le i \le h1‚Č§i‚Č§h, 1‚Č§j‚Č§w1 \le j \le w1‚Č§j‚Č§w) such that Pi,j=Ri,jP_{i, j} = R_{i, j}Pi,j‚Äč=Ri,j‚Äč.

Today, Sasha found two photos with possibly different shapes. She cannot directly compute the similarity of these photos; therefore, she decided to upscale both photos to get two photos with identical shapes. Formally, Sasha can choose two positive integers aaa and bbb and transform a photo with shape (h,w)(h, w)(h,w) into a photo with shape (ah,bw)(ah, bw)(ah,bw), where each pixel from the original photo is replaced by a rectangle of pixels with shape (a,b)(a, b)(a,b) and the same color as that pixel. Since Sasha does not like huge photos, the upscaled photos should be as small as possible (specifically, if the original photos have the same shapes, they should not be changed at all).

Since the resulting photos may be very big, Sasha asks you to help her and compute their similarity.
constraints: each string contains only characters '0' and '1'
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers h1h_1h1‚Äč and w1w_1w1‚Äč denoting the shape of the first photo.
The second line contains a single string with length h1‚čÖw1h_1 \cdot w_1h1‚Äč‚čÖw1‚Äč describing the first photo. For each valid i,ji, ji,j, the pixel in the iii-th row and jjj-th column of the photo is white if the (i‚ąí1)w1+j(i-1)w_1+j(i‚ąí1)w1‚Äč+j-th character of this string is '1' or black if this character is '0'.
The third line contains two space-separated integers h2h_2h2‚Äč and w2w_2w2‚Äč denoting the shape of the second photo.
The fourth line contains a single string describing the second photo in the same format as the first one.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚ÄĒ the similarity of the upscaled photos.
